faqs.answer: We are open Monday to Friday, 9 AM to 6 PM.

faqs.question: How do I contact support?
faqs.answer: You can email us at support@mycompany.com or call +1-234-567-890.

faqs.question: Where are you located?
faqs.answer: We are headquartered in New York, USA.

faqs.question: Hi

faqs.question: What payment methods are accepted?

faqs.question: How long does delivery take?
faqs.answer: Delivery usually takes 3–7 business days depending on your location.

faqs.question: Can I track my order?
faqs.answer: Yes, you will receive a tracking link after your order is shipped.

faqs.answer: Game a b c d e f g h 8 8 7 7 6 6 5 5 4 4 3 3 2 2 1 1 a b c d e f g h Moves 1.e4 e5 ECO C20–C99 Parent King's Pawn Game Synonyms Double King's Pawn Opening Double King's Pawn Game An Open Game (or Double King's Pawn Opening ) is a generic term for a family of chess openings beginning with the moves: 1. e4 e5 Black controls the d4-square in the center and maintains symmetry. 1.e4 e5 gives rise to many of the oldest and most deeply analyzed openings in the game. Play most commonly continues 2.Nf3

faqs.question: [From en.wikipedia.org] Section 5
faqs.answer: Nc6, leading to the Ruy Lopez (3.Bb5), Italian Game (3.Bc4), Scotch Game (3.d4), and others. Black's most important alternative to 2...Nc6 is 2...Nf6, Petrov's Defense . Other Open Games include the King's Gambit (2.f4) and Vienna Game (2.Nc3). After having been the most common pair of opening moves until the 20th century, 1...e5 remains the second most popular response to White's first move 1.e4 ( 1...c5 is slightly more popular among tournament players). [ 1 ] Other responses to 1.e4

faqs.question: [From en.wikipedia.org] Section 6
faqs.answer: are termed Semi-Open Games or Single King's Pawn Games. When written in lowercase, the term "open game" refers to a chess position where ranks , files and diagonals are open, and tending to more tactical gameplay. It is possible that an Open Game may lead to a closed game . [ 2 ] This article uses algebraic notation to describe chess moves. Analysis [ edit ] White opens by playing 1.e4, which is the most popular opening move and has several strengths – it immediately stakes a claim in

faqs.question: [From en.wikipedia.org] Section 7
faqs.answer: the center , and frees two pieces (the queen and king's bishop ) for action. The oldest openings in chess follow 1.e4. Bobby Fischer wrote that 1.e4 is "Best by test." [ 3 ] On the negative side, 1.e4 places a pawn on an undefended square and weakens the squares d4 and f4. If Black keeps the symmetry by replying 1...e5, the result is an Open Game. [ 2 ] [ 4 ] 2.Nf3 Nc6 [ edit ] a b c d e f g h 8 8 7 7 6 6 5 5 4 4 3 3 2 2 1 1 a b c d e f g h King's Knight Opening, main

faqs.question: [From en.wikipedia.org] Section 8
faqs.answer: line: 2.Nf3 Nc6 The most popular second move for White is 2.Nf3 (the King's Knight Opening), attacking Black's king pawn , preparing to castle kingside , and preparing for d2–d4 in many lines. Black's usual reply to 2.Nf3 is 2...Nc6 , defending the king's pawn without restricting the movement of the king's bishop. From here several named openings can arise. The Ruy Lopez or Spanish Game (3.Bb5) is one of the most popular openings at all levels of chess, and one of the most extensively analyzed.

faqs.question: [From en.wikipedia.org] Section 9
faqs.answer: Black's most common response is 3...a6, forcing White to make a decision between exchanging the bishop for the knight with 4.Bxc6 or retreating with 4.Ba4. Also common is 3...Nf6, the Berlin Defense . Black has several alternatives, the sharpest of which is the Jaenisch Gambit or Schliemann Defense, 3...f5. The Italian Game (3.Bc4) is White's second most popular move, usually leading to either the Giuoco Piano (3...Bc5) or the Two Knights Defense (3...Nf6). The name "Italian Game" is also used

faqs.question: [From en.wikipedia.org] Section 10
faqs.answer: as a synonym for the Giuoco Piano by many writers. A sharp continuation to the Giuoco Piano is the Evans Gambit (4.b4), offering a pawn for a lead in development and attacking chances. The Scotch Game (3.d4) was popular in the 19th century and is still the third most common move. After the usual 3...exd4, White may either recapture with 4.Nxd4 with an approximately equal game, or play the Scotch Gambit (4.Bc4), a risky but dangerous tactical line against an unprepared opponent. The Four Knights

faqs.question: [From en.wikipedia.org] Section 11
faqs.answer: Game (3.Nc3 Nf6) was also quite popular in the 19th century. It frequently leads to positional games and is generally considered less challenging for Black to defend against than the three lines above. Less commonly after 3.Nc3, 3....g6 or 3...Bb4 (the Three Knights Game ) are possible. The Ponziani Opening (3.c3) has never achieved widespread popularity but is occasionally used as a surprise weapon. Other third moves for White such as the Konstantinopolsky Opening (3.g3) [ 5 ] [ 6

faqs.question: [From en.wikipedia.org] Section 12
faqs.answer: ] and Inverted Hungarian Opening (3.Be2) are rarely played and offer White no advantage. Other second moves for Black [ edit ] Black's most important alternative to 2...Nc6 is 2...Nf6, known as Petrov's Defense or Russian Defense. It is a common opening in high level play, where it has a reputation as a drawing weapon. White can avoid the extensive theory of the Petrov by playing 3.Nc3, which could transpose into a Four Knights Game after 3...Nc6. Another possibility is 3... Bb4. The

faqs.question: [From en.wikipedia.org] Section 13
faqs.answer: Philidor Defense (2...d6) defends the pawn on e5 but has the disadvantage of restricting the mobility of Black's king's bishop , and typically leads to solid but passive positions for Black. For this reason, it largely fell out of favor by the early 20th century as Black players sought more dynamic options, but it has a dependable reputation and is still occasionally seen at grandmaster level. The Elephant Gambit (2...d5) and the Latvian Gambit (2...f5) are considered very risky for Black. The

faqs.question: [From en.wikipedia.org] Section 14
faqs.answer: Gunderam Defense (2...Qe7) is an offbeat choice which blocks the development of the king's bishop and has never achieved widespread popularity. The Damiano Defense (2...f6) may be met by either 3.Nxe5 or 3.Bc4 with advantage and is almost never seen. The Busch–Gass Gambit (2...Bc5), which was something of an online fad in the early 2020s, is well met by 3.Nxe5 Nc6 4.Nf3. Other second moves for White [ edit ] The most popular alternatives to 2.Nf3 are 2.f4 (the King's Gambit ), 2.Nc3 (the Vienna

faqs.question: [From en.wikipedia.org] Section 15
faqs.answer: Game ), and 2.Bc4 (the Bishop's Opening ). The King's Gambit was popular before the 20th century with master and amateur alike. White offers a pawn to undermine Black's center and achieve quick development. The Vienna Game also frequently features such attacks on the Black center by means of f2–f4. Manual  rental  management  systems  often  face  challenges  such  as  payment 
disputes, inefficient complaint handling, lack of transparency, and reduced trust

faqs.question: [From aichatbot(pdf).pdf] Topic 1
faqs.answer:
Title: AI Chatbot System 
1. Introduction 
An AI Chatbot is a software application that uses Artificial Intelligence to interact with users 
through text or voice. It helps automate conversations and provide instant responses to user 
queries.

faqs.question: [From aichatbot(pdf).pdf] Topic 2
faqs.answer:
2. Objective 
The main objective of this project is: 
• To provide automated responses to users  
• To reduce manual support work  
• To improve user experience

faqs.question: [From aichatbot(pdf).pdf] Topic 3
faqs.answer:
3. Features 
• Answer user questions automatically  
• Supports FAQ-based responses  
• Can learn from website data  
• Can process PDF documents  
• Provides instant replies

faqs.question: [From aichatbot(pdf).pdf] Topic 4
faqs.answer:
4. How It Works 
1. User asks a question  
2. System searches in:  
o FAQ data  
o Knowledge base (URL/PDF)  
3. If answer is found → it replies  
4. If not → AI generates response

faqs.question: [From aichatbot(pdf).pdf] Topic 5
faqs.answer:
5. Technologies Used 
• Frontend: HTML, CSS, JavaScript

faqs.question: [From aichatbot(pdf).pdf] Topic 6
faqs.answer:

• Database: SQLite / JSON  
• AI Integration: API-based chatbot

faqs.question: [From aichatbot(pdf).pdf] Topic 7
faqs.answer:
6. Applications 
• Customer support  
• E-commerce websites  
• Educational platforms  
• Business automation

faqs.question: [From aichatbot(pdf).pdf] Topic 8
faqs.answer:
7. Advantages 
• 24/7 availability  
• Fast responses  
• Reduces human effort  
• Improves efficiency

faqs.question: [From aichatbot(pdf).pdf] Topic 9
faqs.answer:
8. Conclusion 
The AI Chatbot system helps automate communication and improves interaction between 
users and systems. It is a powerful tool for modern applications.

faqs.question: What are the features of an AI chatbot?
faqs.answer: AI chatbot features include answering questions automatically, processing data from websites and PDFs, and providing instant responses.

faqs.question: How does an AI chatbot work?
faqs.answer: It works by searching in FAQ data and knowledge base, and if no data is found, it generates answers using AI.

faqs.question: What are the advantages of AI chatbots?
faqs.answer: AI chatbots provide 24/7 availability, fast responses, and reduce human effort.

faqs.question: What are types of Artificial Intelligence?
faqs.answer: Types of AI include Narrow AI, General AI, and Super AI.

1. Introduction 
An AI Chatbot is a software application that uses Artificial Intelligence to interact with users 
through text or voice. It helps automate conversations and provide instant responses to user 
queries.

• To provide automated responses to users

3. Features 
• Answer user questions automatically  
• Supports FAQ-based responses  
• Can learn from website data  
• Can process PDF documents

1. User asks a question  
2. System searches in:

3. If answer is found → it replies  
4. If not → AI generates response
